esercizi.nome: Move the Knees Sideways While Lying Down
esercizi.description: This movement helps loosen and lubricate the joints of the pelvis and hips, making it easier to get out of bed in the morning.
esercizi.howToDo: Lie on your back on the bed with your knees bent and your feet firmly resting on the mattress. Keeping your shoulders still, slowly let both knees fall to the right, as far as you can comfortably go. Bring them back to the centre, then let them fall to the left, as if your legs were the windscreen wipers of a car. Repeat 5 times on each side.
esercizi.whatToDo: Perform the movement with slow, regular breathing.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not lift your upper back or shoulders off the bed.
esercizi.imageAlt: Move the Knees Sideways While Lying Down

esercizi.nome: Standing Hip Circles with Support
esercizi.description: This movement helps loosen the hip and pelvis joints, improving mobility for walking, turning, and changing direction.
esercizi.howToDo: Stand behind a sturdy chair and hold the backrest with both hands. Keeping your feet on the floor, slowly move your pelvis in a small circle, as if you were drawing a circle with your hips. Make 5 circles in one direction, then 5 circles in the opposite direction.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep the movement small, slow, and comfortable.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not move your shoulders in large circles and do not arch your lower back too much.
esercizi.imageAlt: Standing Hip Circles with Support

esercizi.nome: Standing Weight Shift Side to Side
esercizi.description: This exercise improves balance and trains the pelvis to control body weight when shifting from one leg to the other.
esercizi.howToDo: Stand behind a sturdy chair and hold on with both hands. Keep your feet slightly apart. Slowly shift your weight onto your right foot without lifting the left foot from the floor. Return to the centre, then shift your weight onto your left foot. Repeat 10 times.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep both feet in contact with the floor and move slowly.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not let your hips drop to one side and do not move suddenly.
esercizi.imageAlt: Standing Weight Shift Side to Side

esercizi.nome: Lift Each Side of the Hips Alternately
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the side muscles of the pelvis, which are very useful for regaining balance if you take a wrong step while walking.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit on an upright chair without resting your back against the backrest. Keep your hands on your thighs. Without standing up, lift only your right buttock off the seat, shifting all your weight onto the left side. Hold this position for 2 seconds, place your buttock back down, then lift your left buttock. Alternate the two sides 10 times.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your shoulders as still as possible, moving only the lower part of your pelvis.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not make sudden movements, to avoid losing your balance on the chair.
esercizi.imageAlt: Lift Each Side of the Hips Alternately

esercizi.nome: Move the Leg to the Side While Standing
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the stabilizing muscles of the pelvis. It simulates the movement needed to get into the bathtub, step into the shower, or step over a small obstacle in the street.
esercizi.howToDo: Stand sideways next to a stable chair or table and hold on firmly with your right hand for safety. Keeping your body straight, slightly lift your left leg and move it out to the side, as if you were taking a sideways step in the air. Count to 2, return to the starting position, and repeat the movement 5 times. Then turn around and do the same with your right leg.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep the supporting leg slightly bent to stay more stable.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not lean your whole torso to the side to lift the leg; move only from the hip.
esercizi.imageAlt: Move the Leg to the Side While Standing

esercizi.nome: Supported Side Step
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the side muscles of the pelvis, which are essential for balance and for keeping the body stable while walking.
esercizi.howToDo: Stand behind a sturdy chair or next to a table and hold on with both hands for safety. Starting with your feet together, take a small step to the right with your right foot, then bring your left foot close to it. Repeat the same movement to the left. Move slowly and keep your body upright. Repeat 5 times on each side.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your knees slightly bent and your feet pointing forward during the movement.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not drag your feet and do not lean your torso to the side while stepping.
esercizi.imageAlt: Supported Side Step

esercizi.nome: Supported Cross Step
esercizi.description: This exercise improves coordination and strengthens the muscles that control side movements of the pelvis. It is useful for reacting better when changing direction while walking.
esercizi.howToDo: Stand behind a sturdy chair and hold the backrest with both hands. Slowly cross your right foot in front of your left foot, placing it gently on the floor. Return to the starting position, then cross your left foot in front of your right foot. Alternate the movement slowly for 5 repetitions on each side.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your hands firmly on the chair and perform only small, controlled steps.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not cross your feet too far and do not perform the exercise if you feel unstable.
esercizi.imageAlt: Supported Cross Step

esercizi.nome: Seated Side Toe Tap
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the side muscles of the hip and pelvis in a safe seated position, helping improve control of the legs during side steps.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright on a chair with your feet resting on the floor. Slide your right foot out to the side, keeping the toes in contact with the floor, then bring it back to the centre. Repeat 5 times, then do the same with the left foot.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your trunk upright and move only the leg.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not lean your body to the opposite side while moving the foot.
esercizi.imageAlt: Seated Side Toe Tap

esercizi.nome: Side-Lying Straight Leg Lift
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the lateral hip muscles and improves leg control in a side-lying position, helping pelvic stability during walking.
esercizi.howToDo: Lie on your side on a mat, with your lower leg slightly bent for comfort and your upper leg straight. Slowly lift the upper leg a few centimetres, keeping the toes pointing forward. Hold for 2 seconds, then lower the leg slowly. Repeat 5 times, then turn onto the other side.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your pelvis still and your body aligned.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not roll your body backward and do not lift the leg too high.
esercizi.imageAlt: Side-Lying Straight Leg Lift

esercizi.nome: Push the Leg Back While Standing
esercizi.description: This movement strengthens the posterior muscles of the pelvis, the gluteus maximus muscles, which are essential for maintaining an upright posture and avoiding falling forward while walking.
esercizi.howToDo: Stand facing the backrest of a sturdy chair, or facing a table, and place both hands on it. Keeping your back straight, move your right leg backward without bending the knee, as if you were taking a step backward. Squeeze your buttock muscle for 3 seconds, then bring your foot back next to the other one. Do the same with your left leg.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your gaze straight ahead throughout the exercise.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not arch your lower back forward while the leg moves backward.
esercizi.imageAlt: Push the Leg Back While Standing

esercizi.nome: Backward Toe Tap with Chair Support
esercizi.description: This exercise activates the buttock muscles and improves the ability to move the leg backward safely, which is useful for walking and maintaining an upright posture.
esercizi.howToDo: Stand behind a sturdy chair and hold on with both hands. Move your right foot slightly backward and touch the floor with your toes, without shifting too much weight onto that leg. Return to the starting position and repeat 5 times. Then switch to the left leg.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your chest open and your body straight.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not arch your lower back and do not swing the leg backward quickly.
esercizi.imageAlt: Backward Toe Tap with Chair Support

esercizi.nome: Supported Backward Walking Steps
esercizi.description: This exercise improves coordination and activates the posterior muscles of the pelvis and legs, which are useful for balance and posture.
esercizi.howToDo: Stand beside a stable table or kitchen counter and keep one hand on it for support. Take one small step backward with your right foot, then bring your left foot close to it. Continue for 3 small steps, then return forward carefully. Repeat 3 times.
esercizi.whatToDo: Take very small steps and keep your gaze forward.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not perform this exercise without support and do not rush the backward steps.
esercizi.imageAlt: Supported Backward Walking Steps

esercizi.nome: Backward Lunge with Stick Support
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the buttocks and the back part of the thigh. It helps improve balance and control when stepping backward or adjusting your position while standing.
esercizi.howToDo: Stand upright and hold a stick vertically with one hand for support. Take a small step backward with your right foot, placing the toes gently on the floor. Slightly bend both knees, then push through the front leg to return to the starting position. Repeat 5 times, then switch legs.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your torso upright and perform a small, controlled movement.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not move too quickly and do not place too much weight on the back foot if you feel unstable.
esercizi.imageAlt: Backward Lunge with Stick Support

esercizi.nome: Quadruped Leg Extension
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the buttocks and the back part of the pelvis, helping improve posture and walking stability.
esercizi.howToDo: Get on all fours on a mat. Slowly extend your right leg backward, sliding it along the floor first and then lifting it slightly, keeping the knee straight. Hold for 2 seconds, then return to the starting position. Repeat 5 times, then switch legs.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your pelvis as still as possible while the leg moves.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not arch your lower back and do not lift the leg too high.
esercizi.imageAlt: Quadruped Leg Extension

esercizi.nome: Extend the Leg Forward While Seated
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the front part of the thigh in complete safety, while protecting the knees. It helps build the strength needed to walk with a steady and confident step.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit on an upright chair with your back well supported against the backrest. Keep your hands on the sides of the seat. Slowly lift your right foot and extend your leg forward until it is parallel to the floor. Tighten your thigh muscle well and pull your toes toward you. Count to 3, then slowly lower your foot back down. Do the same with your left leg.
esercizi.whatToDo: Perform the movement very slowly, both when lifting and lowering the leg.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not lift your back away from the chair and do not make quick, jerky movements with your knee.
esercizi.imageAlt: Extend the Leg Forward While Seated

esercizi.nome: Lift the Straight Leg a Few Centimeters While Lying Down
esercizi.description: This movement isolates the muscle at the front of the thigh without placing body weight on the back or joints.
esercizi.howToDo: Lie on your back on the bed. Bend your left knee, placing your foot on the mattress; this helps protect your back. Keep your right leg fully extended on the bed. Tighten the muscle of your right thigh and lift your straight leg only slightly, about the height of one hand. Hold the leg up for 3 seconds, then slowly lower it back down. Repeat 5 times on each side.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your belly slightly pulled in during the lift.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not lift your leg too high and do not arch your back.
esercizi.imageAlt: Lift the Straight Leg a Few Centimeters While Lying Down

esercizi.nome: Half Sit-to-Stand
esercizi.description: This exercise is the most important one for maintaining independence in the legs. It reproduces the daily action of standing up from the toilet, low armchairs, or car seats.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit on the front part of the chair with your feet firmly planted on the floor and slightly apart. Rest your hands on your thighs. Lean your torso slightly forward, push firmly with your thigh muscles, and lift your buttocks off the chair by only two or three fingersâ€™ width. Hold this raised position for 2 seconds, then slowly sit back down. Repeat 5 times.
esercizi.whatToDo: Control the downward movement well, without dropping onto the chair.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not let your knees move inward while pushing.
esercizi.imageAlt: Half Sit-to-Stand

esercizi.nome: Press the Thigh Upward Against the Hands
esercizi.description: This isometric exercise provides considerable strength to the thigh and hip without moving the knee joint, making it excellent for people with osteoarthritis.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright on the chair. Place the palms of both hands on top of your right thigh, just above the knee. Try to lift your thigh toward the ceiling and, at the same time, push firmly downward with your hands to stop the leg from moving. Neither your hands nor your leg should move. Maintain this resistance between your hands and leg for 5 seconds, then relax and switch sides.
esercizi.whatToDo: Breathe out through your mouth during the push.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not round your back forward and do not lift your shoulders.
esercizi.imageAlt: Press the Thigh Upward Against the Hands

esercizi.nome: Mini Squat with Chair Support
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the front part of the thighs and helps improve the ability to stand up, sit down, and walk with more confidence.
esercizi.howToDo: Stand behind a sturdy chair and hold the backrest with both hands. Keep your feet slightly apart. Slowly bend your knees a little, as if you were starting to sit down, then push through your feet to return upright. The movement must be small and controlled. Repeat 5 times.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your knees pointing in the same direction as your toes.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not bend too low and do not let your knees move inward.
esercizi.imageAlt: Mini Squat with Chair Support

esercizi.nome: Seated March with Opposite Arm
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the hip flexors and thighs while improving coordination between the arms and legs, which is useful for walking.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright on the front half of a chair. Lift your right knee slightly while raising your left arm forward at the same time. Lower them slowly, then lift your left knee while raising your right arm. Alternate the movement for 10 repetitions.
esercizi.whatToDo: Move slowly and keep your abdomen slightly engaged.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not lean backward when lifting the knee.
esercizi.imageAlt: Seated March with Opposite Arm

esercizi.nome: Standing Forward Toe Tap
esercizi.description: This exercise trains hip and thigh control, useful for taking a safe step forward or touching the floor before placing full weight on the foot.
esercizi.howToDo: Stand behind a sturdy chair and hold on with both hands. Move your right foot forward and lightly tap the floor with your toes. Bring the foot back to the starting position. Repeat 5 times, then switch legs.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your body upright and your supporting knee slightly bent.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not lean backward while moving the foot forward.
esercizi.imageAlt: Standing Forward Toe Tap

esercizi.nome: Forward Lunge with Stick Support
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the front part of the thigh and improves control when taking a longer step forward, such as when climbing a step or walking over an obstacle.
esercizi.howToDo: Stand upright and hold a stick vertically with one hand for support. Take a small step forward with your right foot. Slowly bend both knees a little, lowering your body only as far as comfortable. Push through the front foot to return to the starting position. Repeat 5 times, then switch legs.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep the front knee aligned with the foot and use the stick to stay stable.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not take a step that is too long and do not let the front knee move inward.
esercizi.imageAlt: Forward Lunge with Stick Support

esercizi.nome: Bend the Knee Back While Standing
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the muscles at the back of the thigh. It simulates the movement needed to lift the foot off the ground to avoid tripping over rugs or pavements.
esercizi.howToDo: Stand behind a sturdy chair or in front of a table and place both hands on it for safety. Keeping your body straight, lift your right foot backward toward your buttocks by bending your knee. Hold still for 2 seconds with the leg bent, then lower your foot back to the floor very slowly. Do the same with your left leg.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your thighs close together and aligned while bending your knee.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not push your thigh forward and do not bend your torso toward the table.
esercizi.imageAlt: Bend the Knee Back While Standing

esercizi.nome: Press the Heel Against the Floor While Seated
esercizi.description: This isometric exercise safely activates the muscles at the back of the thigh, without moving the joints and without tiring the back.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright on the front part of the chair. Move your right foot a small step forward, resting only the heel on the floor, with your toes pointing upward. From this position, push your heel firmly downward against the floor and, at the same time, try to pull it backward toward the chair. The foot should not actually move; it should only apply force. Hold the push for 5 seconds, then switch legs.
esercizi.whatToDo: Feel the muscles under and behind the thigh tighten during the effort.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not hold your breath and do not slide your buttocks forward on the chair.
esercizi.imageAlt: Press the Heel Against the Floor While Seated

esercizi.nome: Press a Soft Ball Under the Knee While Lying Down
esercizi.description: This movement strengthens the back part of the thigh using the bed, making it ideal for reactivating the legs in the morning before getting up.
esercizi.howToDo: Lie on your back on the bed. Place a well-rolled cushion or a soft foam ball directly under your right knee. Your leg will be slightly bent. From this position, firmly push the back of your knee downward to press the cushion against the mattress, slightly lifting your heel. Hold the pressure for 4 seconds, then relax and repeat on the left side.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your hands resting on your belly and breathe normally.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not arch your back and do not tense your neck during the push.
esercizi.imageAlt: Press a Soft Ball Under the Knee While Lying Down

esercizi.nome: Slide the Heel Back on the Floor While Seated
esercizi.description: This exercise simulates the strength needed to move the legs backward or to give yourself a push to adjust your position when sitting on a chair or armchair.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright on the chair. Place the whole sole of your right foot on the floor, slightly farther forward than the chair. Pressing firmly against the floor, slide your foot backward toward the chair, applying strong pressure to the floor. You can use a sock or a cloth under your foot to help it slide more easily. You will feel the back of your thigh working. Repeat 5 times on each side.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your hands firmly on the armrests or on the sides of the chair for stability.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not lift the sole of your foot off the floor while pulling it backward.
esercizi.imageAlt: Slide the Heel Back on the Floor While Seated

esercizi.nome: Seated Pillow Squeeze Under the Thigh
esercizi.description: This exercise gently activates the back of the thigh while seated, helping improve leg stability without loading the knee too much.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright on a chair. Place a small soft cushion or folded towel under your right thigh, close to the back of the knee. Press your thigh downward into the cushion for 5 seconds, then relax. Repeat 5 times, then switch legs.
esercizi.whatToDo: Feel the back of the thigh working during the pressure.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not lift your foot from the floor and do not lean your trunk backward.
esercizi.imageAlt: Seated Pillow Squeeze Under the Thigh

esercizi.nome: Supported Step Over an Imaginary Line
esercizi.description: This exercise trains the ability to lift the foot and clear small obstacles, helping reduce the risk of tripping.
esercizi.howToDo: Stand behind a sturdy chair and hold on with both hands. Imagine a line on the floor in front of your right foot. Lift your right foot and step over the imaginary line, then bring it back to the starting position. Repeat 5 times, then switch legs.
esercizi.whatToDo: Lift the foot clearly from the floor and place it down softly.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not drag the foot across the floor and do not lean heavily on the chair.
esercizi.imageAlt: Supported Step Over an Imaginary Line

esercizi.nome: Kneeling Rock Back on the Mat
esercizi.description: This movement gently mobilizes the hips and knees, helping reduce stiffness and improve comfort when bending the legs.
esercizi.howToDo: Get on all fours on a mat. Keeping your hands on the floor, slowly move your pelvis backward toward your heels, only as far as comfortable. Then return slowly to the starting position. Repeat 5 times.
esercizi.whatToDo: Move slowly and stop before feeling pain in the knees or hips.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not force your pelvis all the way back if the knees feel uncomfortable.
esercizi.imageAlt: Kneeling Rock Back on the Mat

esercizi.nome: Heel Slide on the Bed
esercizi.description: This movement trains knee flexion gently and without placing body weight on the joint, helping keep the joint well lubricated.
esercizi.howToDo: Lie on your back on the bed with your legs extended. Slowly slide your right heel along the mattress, bringing it toward your buttocks so that you bend your knee as far as you can without feeling pain. Then slide your heel forward to return to the starting position. Repeat 5 times for each leg.
esercizi.whatToDo: You can use a wool sock or a cloth under your heel to help it slide more easily.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not force the bend if you feel pain, and do not lift your heel off the bed during the movement.
esercizi.imageAlt: Heel Slide on the Bed

esercizi.nome: Heel Lift While Lying on the Stomach
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the posterior muscles surrounding the knee. It helps older adults build the strength needed to bend the leg when stepping onto a high step or putting on trousers.
esercizi.howToDo: Lie on your stomach on the bed in a comfortable position. If your back hurts, you can place a flat cushion under your abdomen. From this position, slowly bend your right knee, bringing your heel toward your buttocks as far as you can without feeling pain. Hold the leg bent for 3 seconds, then lower your foot back down onto the bed very slowly. Repeat 5 times, then switch legs.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your pelvis and hips well supported on the mattress while lifting your foot.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not make fast or jerky movements and do not arch your back.
esercizi.imageAlt: Heel Lift While Lying on the Stomach

esercizi.nome: Seated Pendulum
esercizi.description: This exercise uses gravity to let the knee move freely. It helps reduce joint stiffness, which is common after spending a lot of time sitting in an armchair.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit on a chair that is high enough so your feet do not fully touch the floor, or use a small support under the chair. Let your legs hang freely. Very slowly, swing your right leg forward and backward, as if it were the pendulum of a clock. Continue this movement for 10 seconds, then do the same with your left leg.
esercizi.whatToDo: Completely relax the leg muscles; the movement should be very light and effortless.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not make wide or fast swings if you feel discomfort or pain.
esercizi.imageAlt: Seated Pendulum

esercizi.nome: Seated Knee Circles
esercizi.description: This exercise helps keep the knee joint mobile and gently lubricated, reducing stiffness after sitting for a long time.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright on a chair with your feet resting on the floor. Lift your right foot slightly from the ground and draw small circles in the air with your knee. Make 5 slow circles in one direction, then 5 in the opposite direction. Place the foot down and repeat with the left leg.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep the movement small and relaxed.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not make large or fast circles if you feel discomfort in the knee.
esercizi.imageAlt: Seated Knee Circles

esercizi.nome: Straighten the Knee with a Pillow Under the Thigh
esercizi.description: This exercise helps restore full knee extension. It is very useful for regaining the correct leg push when walking, helping prevent limping.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit on the bed or on a comfortable sofa with your legs stretched out in front of you. Place a rolled towel or a small cushion under your right thigh, just above the knee. Push the back of your thigh downward against the cushion and, at the same time, straighten your knee by lifting your heel off the bed. Keep your leg straight for 4 seconds, then slowly lower it back down.
esercizi.whatToDo: Pull your toes toward your face as your leg straightens.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not make sudden movements and do not lift your thigh off the cushion.
esercizi.imageAlt: Straighten the Knee with a Pillow Under the Thigh

esercizi.nome: Seated Knee Straightening with Heel on the Floor
esercizi.description: This exercise helps regain knee extension in a gentle way, useful for walking with a more stable step.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright on a chair. Place your right heel on the floor slightly in front of you, with your toes pointing upward. Slowly straighten your knee as much as comfortable, gently tightening the front of your thigh. Hold for 3 seconds, then relax. Repeat 5 times and switch legs.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your heel on the floor while straightening the knee.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not force the knee fully straight if it causes pain.
esercizi.imageAlt: Seated Knee Straightening with Heel on the Floor

esercizi.nome: Rise onto the Toes
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the calves. It simulates the movement used to reach up and take an object from a high shelf or to look over an obstacle, improving balance.
esercizi.howToDo: Stand behind the backrest of a sturdy chair, or in front of a table, and place both hands on it for safety. Keeping your body straight, slowly lift your heels off the floor, rising up onto your toes. Stay up for 2 seconds, then slowly lower yourself back down, placing the whole sole of your foot on the floor. Repeat 10 times.
esercizi.whatToDo: Push the top of your head toward the ceiling as you rise, keeping your body straight.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not make jerky movements and do not suddenly drop down onto your heels during the lowering phase.
esercizi.imageAlt: Rise onto the Toes

esercizi.nome: Wall Calf Stretch
esercizi.description: This exercise stretches the calf muscles and helps maintain ankle mobility, which is important for walking and climbing stairs.
esercizi.howToDo: Stand facing a wall and place both hands on it at chest height. Step your right foot slightly backward, keeping the heel on the floor and the knee straight. Bend the front knee a little until you feel a gentle stretch in the back calf. Hold for 5 seconds, then switch legs.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep the back heel firmly on the floor.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not bounce during the stretch and do not force the movement if you feel pain.
esercizi.imageAlt: Wall Calf Stretch

esercizi.nome: Seated Calf Press into the Floor
esercizi.description: This isometric exercise strengthens the calf muscles without requiring standing balance.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright on a chair with both feet flat on the floor. Press the front part of your right foot firmly into the floor, as if you wanted to rise onto your toes without actually lifting the heel. Hold the pressure for 5 seconds, then relax. Repeat with the left foot.
esercizi.whatToDo: Feel the calf muscle tighten during the pressure.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not lift the heel if the goal is to keep the movement isometric.
esercizi.imageAlt: Seated Calf Press into the Floor

esercizi.nome: Seated Heel Walking
esercizi.description: This movement strengthens the front part of the lower leg, the shin. It helps build the strength needed to lift the toes properly when walking, preventing trips over rugs or pavement edges.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright on a chair with your feet resting on the floor. Keeping your heels firmly planted on the floor, lift the toes of both feet upward at the same time, bringing them as close as possible to your shins. Hold your toes lifted for 3 seconds, feeling the front part of your lower leg working, then place your feet back down. Repeat 10 times.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your hands resting on your thighs and your back away from the backrest.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not lift your heels off the floor and do not hold your breath.
esercizi.imageAlt: Seated Heel Walking

esercizi.nome: Seated Pedaling Movement
esercizi.description: This exercise combines toe and heel movement. It works like a real blood pump, which is very useful for reducing swelling in the legs and heavy ankles at the end of the day.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit comfortably on the chair. Slightly lift your right foot off the floor, keeping your leg slightly extended. Point your toes forward, as if you were pressing the car pedal, then pull your toes backward toward your face, bringing your heel forward. Repeat this continuous movement 10 times, then do the same with your left foot.
esercizi.whatToDo: Move your ankle in a very smooth and wide range of motion.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not move your knee up and down; the movement should come only from the ankle.
esercizi.imageAlt: Seated Pedaling Movement

esercizi.nome: Press the Feet Outward Against the Chair Legs
esercizi.description: This isometric exercise strengthens the side muscles of the lower leg, which are directly responsible for ankle stability when walking on uneven ground, such as cobblestones or grass.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit on the chair and place your feet inside the front legs of the chair, so that the outer side of your feet touches the wood or metal of the chair. Push your feet firmly outward against the chair legs, as if you were trying to spread them apart. Maintain this steady push for 5 seconds, then relax.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your hands firmly on the seat to help you apply force.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not lift the soles of your feet off the floor during the push.
esercizi.imageAlt: Press the Feet Outward Against the Chair Legs

esercizi.nome: Draw Numbers in the Air with the Foot
esercizi.description: This exercise trains ankle mobility in all directions. It helps keep the joint flexible and ready to react to changes in direction.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright on a chair. Lift your right foot off the floor, keeping your leg slightly extended forward. Imagine that your big toe is a pencil and use it to â€œdrawâ€ the numbers from 1 to 5 in the air with slow, large movements. Place your foot back down and do the same with your left ankle.
esercizi.whatToDo: Move only your ankle, keeping your knee and thigh as still as possible.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not rush; the slower the movements are, the more effective the exercise will be.
esercizi.imageAlt: Draw Numbers in the Air with the Foot

esercizi.nome: Press the Soles of the Feet Together
esercizi.description: This isometric exercise strengthens the inner muscles of the ankle, which are essential for preventing the foot from collapsing inward when walking.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit on the front part of the chair with your knees slightly apart. Bring the soles of your feet together, so that the inner sides of your feet touch. Gently push your feet against each other, as if you were trying to squeeze something between them. Maintain the steady push for 5 seconds, then relax.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your hands resting on the sides of the chair to stay upright and stable.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not lift your heels off the floor during the effort.
esercizi.imageAlt: Press the Soles of the Feet Together

esercizi.nome: Lift the Outside of the Foot While Keeping the Heel Still
esercizi.description: This movement strengthens the outer muscles of the ankle, which act as true protective shields against sprains and sideways falls.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit with your feet firmly planted on the floor. Keeping your heels fixed on the floor, try to lift only the outer edge of your right foot upward, so the little toe side moves toward the ceiling while the big toe stays close to the floor. Hold the position for 3 seconds, then slowly lower it back down and repeat with your left foot.
esercizi.whatToDo: Feel the muscles working on the outer side of the lower leg and ankle.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not lift your heels off the floor and do not rotate your knees.
esercizi.imageAlt: Lift the Outside of the Foot While Keeping the Heel Still

esercizi.nome: Rock Between Toes and Heels
esercizi.description: This functional exercise trains dynamic ankle balance, simulating the continuous weight shift that happens when walking or suddenly stopping.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit on the front part of a chair, with your back away from the backrest. Slowly lift your heels, rising onto your toes. Slowly lower them back down and, without stopping, lift your toes, shifting onto your heels. Continue this slow, controlled rocking movement 5 times.
esercizi.whatToDo: Slightly tighten your abdominal and buttock muscles to keep your body straight like a board.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not push your buttocks backward when shifting onto your heels.
esercizi.imageAlt: Rock Between Toes and Heels

esercizi.nome: Standing Heel-to-Toe Weight Shift
esercizi.description: This exercise trains dynamic ankle control and helps improve balance during walking, especially when slowing down or changing speed.
esercizi.howToDo: Stand behind a sturdy chair and hold on with both hands. Slowly shift your body weight forward onto your toes, lifting your heels slightly from the floor. Then slowly shift your weight backward onto your heels, lifting the front part of your feet slightly. Continue this gentle rocking movement for 5 repetitions.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your body tall and move slowly from the ankles.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not let yourself fall backward or forward; always keep control of the movement.
esercizi.imageAlt: Standing Heel-to-Toe Weight Shift

esercizi.nome: Seated Inner Foot Lift
esercizi.description: This movement strengthens the muscles that control the inner side of the ankle, helping improve foot alignment during walking.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit with your feet flat on the floor. Keeping your heel still, try to lift only the inner edge of your right foot slightly upward, so the big toe side moves away from the floor. Hold for 2 seconds, then lower it slowly. Repeat 5 times, then switch feet.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep the heel planted on the floor during the movement.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not rotate your knee inward or lift the whole foot.
esercizi.imageAlt: Seated Inner Foot Lift

esercizi.nome: Curl a Tissue with the Toes
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the small muscles under the sole of the foot. It helps provide stability and grip on the ground when walking barefoot or with shoes.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit on an upright chair. Spread a cloth handkerchief, or a piece of kitchen paper, on the floor in front of your right foot. Place your bare foot, or your foot with a sock on, over the handkerchief. Using only your toes, scrunch and pull the cloth toward your heel. Repeat 3 times, then switch feet.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your heel firmly resting on the floor; only your toes should move.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not lift your whole foot off the floor to move the handkerchief.
esercizi.imageAlt: Curl a Tissue with the Toes

esercizi.nome: Spread the Toes Like a Fan
esercizi.description: This movement helps create more space between the toes, counteracting stiffness caused by tight shoes and improving the bodyâ€™s base of support.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit comfortably on the chair with your feet firmly resting on the floor. Slightly lift the toes of your right foot while keeping the sole of your foot on the floor. Spread your toes apart as much as possible, as if you were trying to form a fan. Hold the open position for 3 seconds, then relax your toes and place them back on the floor. Repeat 5 times on each side.
esercizi.whatToDo: Preferably do the exercise without shoes, so you can clearly see the movement.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not tense your neck or hands while trying to spread your toes.
esercizi.imageAlt: Spread the Toes Like a Fan

esercizi.nome: Massage the Sole of the Foot with a Small Ball
esercizi.description: This exercise reactivates the foot receptors that send balance information to the brain, while also releasing painful tension under the sole of the foot.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright. Place a tennis ball, or a hard rubber ball, under the sole of your right foot. Slide your foot forward and backward over the ball, applying gentle pressure, from the heel to the toes. Continue this slow massage for 15 seconds, then switch to the left foot.
esercizi.whatToDo: Adjust the pressure of your foot so that the massage feels pleasant and never painful.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not do this exercise while standing, to avoid slipping or losing your balance.
esercizi.imageAlt: Massage the Sole of the Foot with a Small Ball

esercizi.nome: Lift Only the Big Toe and Then the Other Toes
esercizi.description: This movement trains fine foot control, which is very useful for providing the correct push-off at the end of each step when walking.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit with your feet flat on the floor. Try to lift only the big toe of your right foot upward, keeping the other four toes firmly pressed against the floor. Then keep the big toe down and try to do the opposite: lift the other four toes while keeping the big toe still on the floor. Repeat this slow alternation 5 times, then switch feet.
esercizi.whatToDo: If at first your toes do not move on their own, you can help yourself by looking at them and concentrating.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not lift the inner or outer edge of the foot; the sole should remain still.
esercizi.imageAlt: Lift Only the Big Toe and Then the Other Toes

esercizi.nome: Seated Toe Press into the Floor
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the toes and improves the footâ€™s ability to grip the ground during walking.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit on a chair with your feet resting on the floor. Press all the toes of your right foot gently into the floor, as if you wanted to grip the ground without curling them too much. Hold for 5 seconds, then relax. Repeat 5 times and switch feet.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep the heel and the ball of the foot resting on the floor.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not curl the toes so strongly that they cramp.
esercizi.imageAlt: Seated Toe Press into the Floor

esercizi.nome: Seated Big Toe Press
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the big toe, which is important for balance and for the final push-off phase of walking.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit with your feet flat on the floor. Keep the right foot still and press only the big toe downward into the floor. Hold the pressure for 3 seconds, then relax. Repeat 5 times, then switch feet.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep the other toes relaxed while the big toe works.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not lift the heel or twist the foot during the pressure.
esercizi.imageAlt: Seated Big Toe Press
